summary:
Prophylactic antibiotics reduce the risk of SSI in clean-contaminated surgery by about 1-2%, based on current evidence from multiple trials. This supports a low but non-negligible rate as indicated in option A. \boxed{A}

The summary captures that prophylaxis reduces SSIs significantly yet keeps within observed narrow bounds while emphasizing relevance directly tied back to provided options. It also maintains focus strictly on pertinent clinical outcomes without extraneous details.
